tasks.description: Add infrastructure arch module: create libs/infrastructure/src/arch.rs (or arch/mod.rs) with ArchRulesError enum (Io/Parse/InvalidRules variants + Debug/Display/Error impls), and four free functions render_workspace_tree, render_workspace_tree_full, render_workspace_members, render_direct_checks — each taking &std::path::Path (workspace root) and returning Result<String, ArchRulesError>. Implement workspace tree rendering (crate-only and with extra_dirs), members listing (one path per line), and direct-check matrix (crate TAB forbidden|crates) matching Python output format exactly (CN-02/IN-10). Register the new module in libs/infrastructure/src/lib.rs. Add Rust unit tests covering: tree rendering with known architecture-rules.json fixture, members listing, direct-checks output format parity, error cases (missing file, invalid JSON). No CLI wiring yet — infrastructure layer only.

Add infrastructure conventions module: create libs/infrastructure/src/conventions.rs (or conventions/mod.rs) with ConventionDocsError enum (Io/MissingReadme/MissingMarkers/AlreadyExists/InvalidSlug variants + Debug/Display/Error impls) and three free functions: add_convention_doc (creates template file + calls update_convention_index), update_convention_index (regenerates README.md index block between markers), verify_convention_index (relocates existing logic from libs/infrastructure/src/verify/convention_docs.rs; the existing function body is moved, not rewritten — CN-06). Keep libs/infrastructure/src/verify/convention_docs.rs as a minimal compatibility shim whose verify function delegates to conventions::verify_convention_index, and keep its pub mod entry in libs/infrastructure/src/verify/mod.rs until T004 rewires the CLI composition caller; do not leave duplicate behavior. Register the new module in libs/infrastructure/src/lib.rs. Add Rust unit tests covering: template generation, index update idempotency, verify pass/fail cases (existing tests from convention_docs.rs are migrated here). No CLI wiring yet — infrastructure layer only.

Add sotp arch subcommand group: create apps/cli/src/commands/arch.rs with ArchCommand enum (Tree/TreeFull/Members/DirectChecks) and execute fn, each variant delegating to the corresponding CliApp method; add the module to apps/cli/src/commands/mod.rs and wire CliCommand::Arch in apps/cli/src/main.rs. Create apps/cli-composition/src/arch.rs with CliApp methods arch_tree, arch_tree_full, arch_members, arch_direct_checks that call the infrastructure functions from T001; register in apps/cli-composition/src/lib.rs. All four subcommands accept --project-root (default '.') and print the returned string to stdout (exit 0 on success, exit 1 on error). Add integration-level tests (in apps/cli/src/commands/arch.rs #[cfg(test)]) verifying command dispatch roundtrip on a temp directory with a minimal architecture-rules.json. Depends on T001.

Add sotp conventions subcommand group and update verify wiring: create apps/cli/src/commands/conventions.rs with ConventionsCommand enum (Add/UpdateIndex/VerifyIndex) and execute fn; add the module to apps/cli/src/commands/mod.rs and wire CliCommand::Conventions in apps/cli/src/main.rs. Create apps/cli-composition/src/conventions.rs with CliApp methods conventions_add (name/slug/title/summary params), conventions_update_index, conventions_verify_index that call the infrastructure functions from T002. Update apps/cli/src/commands/verify.rs and apps/cli-composition verify wiring: replace the verify::ConventionDocs dispatch arm (which previously called infrastructure::verify::convention_docs::verify through the T002 compatibility shim) to call infrastructure::conventions::verify_convention_index instead — preserving sotp verify convention-docs (or its equivalent) as a passthrough entry point. After the dispatch no longer needs the shim, delete libs/infrastructure/src/verify/convention_docs.rs and remove its pub mod entry from libs/infrastructure/src/verify/mod.rs. Add integration-level tests in apps/cli/src/commands/conventions.rs #[cfg(test)]. Depends on T002.

Atomic Python removal + CI/verification sync (CN-03, CN-04): (1) Delete scripts/ directory entirely (all .py, requirements-python.txt, ruff.toml files — IN-03, IN-08, AC-01). (2) Edit Dockerfile to remove python3/python3-yaml/python3-pytest apt install lines and uv-based requirements-python.txt install step (IN-04, AC-02). (3) Edit Makefile.toml: delete python-lint, python-lint-local, scripts-selftest, scripts-selftest-local tasks; remove venv bootstrap step from bootstrap task; remove python-lint-local and scripts-selftest-local from ci-local and ci-merge-local dependency lists; in the same change, resolve every Python-backed architecture/convention wrapper task (workspace-tree, workspace-tree-full, architecture-rules-workspace-members, architecture-rules-direct-checks, architecture-rules-verify-sync, conventions-add, conventions-update-index, conventions-verify-index) by either deleting the non-git passthrough wrapper per the cargo-make teardown ADR or rewriting it to call bin/sotp arch ... / bin/sotp conventions ... with no python3 dependency (IN-05, AC-03). (4) Edit libs/infrastructure/src/verify/orchestra.rs so EXPECTED_CARGO_MAKE_ALLOW, forbidden command checks, and deny entries match the Makefile.toml state from step (3): remove python-lint/scripts-selftest entries, remove wrapper-task allow entries for wrappers deleted in step (3), retain/update any entries for wrappers kept as Rust-native delegates, add new bin/sotp arch and bin/sotp conventions permissions as appropriate, remove .cache/pytest/ deny entries and python3:/pytest: FORBIDDEN_ALLOW entries that will no longer apply, and remove .cache/uv deny entries if uv is no longer used (IN-06, CN-04). (5) Edit libs/infrastructure/src/verify/doc_patterns.rs in the same change to remove checks for deleted Python wrapper task mentions and align verify-arch-docs with the new native entrypoints (CN-04, AC-10 partial). (6) Edit .claude/settings.json permissions.allow to mirror the step (3) wrapper decision: remove Bash(cargo make python-lint), Bash(cargo make scripts-selftest), remove Bash(cargo make architecture-rules-*/workspace-tree*/conventions-*) entries only for wrappers deleted in Makefile.toml, keep or update entries for any Rust-native wrapper that remains callable, and add Bash(bin/sotp arch ...) and Bash(bin/sotp conventions ...) entries. All of (1)-(6) must be in the same commit (CN-04). CI must pass after this commit — especially verify-orchestra and verify-arch-docs. Depends on T001, T002, T003, T004.

Downstream docs/commands/skills/agents update (AC-10): With Makefile.toml wrapper deletion/rewrite decisions already completed in T005, update downstream prose references only: scan DEVELOPER_AI_WORKFLOW.md, track/workflow.md, .claude/rules/07-dev-environment.md, CLAUDE.md, .claude/commands/**, .claude/skills/**, .claude/agents/** for references to deleted cargo make Python wrapper tasks and update them to the surviving Rust-native wrapper or direct bin/sotp arch ... / bin/sotp conventions ... entrypoint chosen in T005. Do not defer any Makefile.toml or orchestra/settings permission synchronization from T005 into this task. CI must pass after this commit. Depends on T005.

Roadmap ADR back-reference (IN-09, AC-09): Edit knowledge/adr/2026-04-13-1200-scripts-python-helpers-rust-migration-roadmap.md — append a back-reference note to the Phase 3 section body stating that this ADR's remaining work (convention_docs / architecture_rules command migration and scripts/ deletion) is completed by knowledge/adr/2026-06-03-1327-python-runtime-full-removal.md. Do NOT modify the front-matter (no status/superseded_by changes — OS-04). This is a minimal prose addition only. CI must pass after this commit.

summary: Known generated-view deviation for this review: contract-map.md currently renders update_convention_index and verify_convention_index with the same F82 length prefix in their Mermaid node ids. This is a contract-map-renderer issue, not plan content, and the renderer fix is tracked separately.

Known generated-view deviation for this review: infrastructure-types.md signal columns reflect implementation progress. All infrastructure catalogue types now render blue (0 yellow). T003-T007 types are CLI/other layer types not tracked in the infrastructure catalogue.

sections.title: Infrastructure layer additions (architecture rules + convention docs)
description: Add the two new infrastructure modules that implement the Rust-side logic for the Python commands being replaced. T001 is additive; T002 relocates the existing convention index verifier into the new conventions module while keeping a temporary verify::convention_docs compatibility shim, so both tasks can be written and tested independently before the broader Python runtime removal.

T001 implements the four sotp arch free functions (render_workspace_tree, render_workspace_tree_full, render_workspace_members, render_direct_checks) with ArchRulesError. T002 implements the three sotp conventions free functions (add_convention_doc, update_convention_index, verify_convention_index) with ConventionDocsError, including the relocation of verify::convention_docs::verify (CN-06).

Both tasks include Rust unit tests as required by CN-05. The <500 line target is achievable for each: the four arch functions plus tests fit in one module; the three conventions functions plus migrated tests fit in one module.

sections.title: CLI subcommand groups (sotp arch + sotp conventions)
description: Wire the infrastructure functions from S1 into the CLI layer as sotp arch and sotp conventions subcommand groups.

T003 adds apps/cli/src/commands/arch.rs, the CliCommand::Arch variant, and composition wiring for the four arch subcommands. T004 adds apps/cli/src/commands/conventions.rs, the CliCommand::Conventions variant, composition wiring for add/update-index/verify-index, updates the verify dispatch to delegate to the relocated verify_convention_index, and removes the temporary verify::convention_docs compatibility shim once the caller no longer needs it.

After S2, bin/sotp arch tree / tree-full / members / direct-checks and bin/sotp conventions add / update-index / verify-index are all functional. Each task includes integration-level roundtrip tests (CN-05).

sections.title: Atomic Python removal and CI/verification sync
description: The single largest commit of the track. Deletes scripts/ entirely (IN-03, IN-08), strips Python from Dockerfile (IN-04), removes Python-specific Makefile.toml tasks and CI dependencies, resolves all Python-backed architecture/convention wrapper tasks by deleting or rewriting them to Rust-native entrypoints (IN-05, AC-03), and simultaneously updates orchestra.rs, doc_patterns.rs, and settings.json so that the permission allowlist, deny list, documentation-pattern checks, and verification checks match the post-Python state (IN-06, CN-04).

CN-04 forbids splitting scripts/ deletion from CI/verification logic updates — all six sub-steps (scripts/ delete, Dockerfile, Makefile.toml, orchestra.rs, doc_patterns.rs, settings.json) must land in the same commit. CI passes after this commit because the Rust commands from S2 are already in place and the Python wiring they replace has been surgically removed.

sections.title: Makefile entrypoint sync and downstream doc update
description: With Makefile.toml wrapper deletion/rewrite decisions already completed in T005, sweep downstream prose consumers (slash commands, skills, agents, workflow docs, DEVELOPER_AI_WORKFLOW.md, CLAUDE.md, .claude/rules/07-dev-environment.md) to remove references to deleted Python-backed cargo make tasks and update to the Rust-native wrapper or direct bin/sotp native entrypoint chosen in T005 (AC-10).
No Makefile.toml or orchestra/settings permission synchronization is deferred into this task; T006 is documentation and command-reference cleanup after the CI-sensitive removal commit.

sections.title: Roadmap ADR back-reference
description: Append the back-reference note to the Phase 3 section of the roadmap ADR (knowledge/adr/2026-04-13-1200-scripts-python-helpers-rust-migration-roadmap.md) recording that this ADR's work completes the Phase 3 remaining tasks (IN-09, AC-09). No front-matter changes (OS-04). This is a standalone commit that can land after the main implementation is CI-green.
